questions.question: What do Brian's expectations of rescue reveal about how he views his current setting?

responses.content: paragraph 4 says"probably come with amphibious planes,small bush planes with floats that could land right here on the lake and pick him up and take him home'
the plane could land on the lake and try to find Brian and help him get home

questions.question: Reread paragraphs 7-9. Why does Brian evaluate his surroundings so carefully?

responses.content: Brian wonders when the plane will find him and help him
that if the pale found him it would pick him up and help him get back home
Brian pictures the plane landing on the lake and the pepole on the plane would try to find brian and help him and get him home as soon as possible

questions.question: How does the setting in paragraphs 12-14 cause Brian to change from feeling hopeless to taking action?

responses.content: he wishes he had food becuase he is very hungery and their is no food around him

questions.question: This part of the story takes place in the wilderness. What challenge does this setting create for Brian?

responses.content: C. The setting's lack of food sources forces Brian to confront the reality of his survival needs

questions.question: Which detail from the text best supports the answer to the previous question?

responses.content: C. “The lake water had filled his stomach but left it hungry”
